diagrams.title: ReLU Activation Function Graph
diagrams.description: A 2D coordinate plane showing the ReLU (Rectified Linear Unit) function formula f(x) = max(0, x). The graph displays a horizontal line at y=0 for x<0, then transitions to a 45-degree line for x>=0. This visualization helps learners understand how ReLU zeroes out negative values while passing positive values unchanged, a key characteristic that enables efficient training of deep neural networks.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the mathematical definition and visual shape of the ReLU activation function, demonstrating how it introduces non-linearity while being computationally efficient

diagrams.title: Softmax Function Probability Distribution
diagrams.description: A diagram showing the Softmax function transformation from raw score inputs to normalized probability outputs. Display example input values (logits) like [2.0, 1.0, 0.1] and their corresponding Softmax probabilities that sum to 1.0. The diagram includes an arrow or transformation visualization showing how higher scores get proportionally higher probabilities through the exponential normalization.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how Softmax converts neural network output scores into probability distributions for multi-class classification, explaining the exponential normalization that amplifies differences between scores

diagrams.title: ReLU vs Softmax in Neural Network Context
diagrams.description: A simplified neural network layer diagram showing where ReLU and Softmax are typically applied. The diagram displays hidden layers with ReLU activations (shown as ReLU nodes) and the output layer with Softmax activation for classification. Visual annotations indicate that ReLU is used in hidden layers for efficient gradient flow, while Softmax is used in the output layer to produce interpretable class probabilities.
diagrams.purpose: Provides context for when and why each activation function is used in deep learning architectures, clarifying that ReLU enables deep networks to train effectively while Softmax produces proper probability outputs for classification tasks
